fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Vermont Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Vermont - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity vermont, how to transfer dietitian license to vermont, dietitian compact vermont, vermont dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Dietitians
board.mailing_address:
Office of Professional Regulation
89 Main Street, 3rd Floor
Montpelier, VT 05620-3402

license_types.title: Certified Dietitian (CD)
license_types.description: Vermont operates under a certification model rather than strict licensure for dietitians. The 'Certified Dietitian' title is protected.

steps.title: Create an OPR Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the My OPRB Online Services portal (secure.vermont.gov/myvoprb/) by providing your email and creating a password, then answering security questions.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log into your My OPRB Online Services account and select the 'Initial Dietitian License Application' to complete and submit all required sections of the application.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts confirming your qualifying degree, verification of your supervised practice hours, and official scores from the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) examination to be sent directly to the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the required $100 initial license application fee through the My OPRB Online Services portal as part of your application submission.

steps.title: Await Review and Approval
steps.description: The Office of Professional Regulation will review your complete application and submitted documents. You will be notified of the licensing decision after the review process.

documents.detail: Application submitted electronically with the required fee.
Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

provisional_text: Vermont does not explicitly mandate full licensure for all nutrition service providers but does protect the 'Certified Dietitian' title, which requires the CDR components to be met first.
reciprocity_text: Applicants currently certified or licensed as a dietitian in good standing in another jurisdiction can apply for certification in Vermont via the online system if their jurisdiction's requirements are substantially equivalent to Vermont's (typically demonstrated by active CDR registration).

faqs.question: How much does a Vermont dietitian certification cost?
faqs.answer: The initial cost is a $100 application fee.

faqs.question: When does the Vermont certification need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Vermont dietitian certifications must be renewed every two years. The renewal fee is $100.

faqs.question: Is a license required to practice dietetics in Vermont?
faqs.answer: Vermont uses a certification model, protecting the title 'Certified Dietitian'. While individuals can provide certain nutrition services without certification, only those meeting state standards and obtaining certification can use the CD title.

faqs.question: Does Vermont have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Vermont. To work in Vermont, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Vermont?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Vermont while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Applicants currently certified or licensed as a dietitian in good standing in another jurisdiction can apply for certification in Vermont via the online system if their jurisdiction's requirements are substantially equivalent to Vermont's (typically demonstrated by active CDR registration).

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Vermont
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian license in Vermont, navigate to the My OPRB Online Services portal, register for an account, and complete the initial license application.

official_links.registration_steps: Create an account by clicking "New User? Register here" on the My OPRB Online Services portal login page. Provide an email, create a password, and set up security questions. Once registered, log in to access and complete the online application for a license.
